turns.content: I'm studying for my interview and I keep thinking about panics when they happen in foreign functions. It feels a lot like how C++ handles exceptions, except Rust doesn't have a try-catch syntax so it seems like catching errors is impossible.

Actually you can catch them if the function implements UnwindSafe or you wrap it in AssertUnwindSafe. For example std::panic::catch_unwind will panic again unless you use AssertUnwindSafe because RefCell and pointers like *mut T are not UnwindSafe by default.
So I should just wrap any foreign function call in AssertUnwindSafe to make it safe from panics?
Not quite. While you can use AssertUnwindSafe at FFI boundaries, it is not a proof of correctness and does not guarantee safety if the underlying code actually panics.
